plan.objective: Build a replayable, stage-gated AI support pipeline that reads tickets.json and policy_kb.json from disk, triages each ticket with a per-ticket LLM call, retrieves relevant policy snippets deterministically in code, drafts a reply with a second per-ticket LLM call constrained to cite only retrieved policies, runs deterministic guardrail checks, and emits a final reviewed response pack — enforced through an explicit state machine and fully regenerable from a clean checkout with zero secrets. THIS IS A 60-MINUTE BOX. Success = MUST items 1–5 produce correct artifacts that would pass the evaluator's own checks on both the sample fixtures AND a swapped fixture. A working, validating MVP beats completeness; do not start SHOULD/STRETCH until MVP is green.
plan.time_budget:
LIMIT: 60 minutes. MVP-first, ruthless. MVP must be green at 0:56.
- 0:00–0:03 (3m) Brainstorm skill: ONE fast batch of open questions + confirm assumptions. If unanswered in ~1 min, PROCEED on the defaults in . Do not stall. - 0:03–0:15 (12m) Scaffold repo, config/settings.json, io_utils, Stage enum + StateMachine, provider base + MockProvider, prompt_hash + llm_calls.jsonl logging. - 0:15–0:25 (10m) Stage 1 triage: per-ticket calls -> triage.json. - 0:25–0:34 (9m) Deterministic retrieval (keyword/tag overlap) -> retrieval_results.json. - 0:34–0:44 (10m) Stage 2 drafting: per-ticket, cites ONLY retrieved -> draft_responses.json. - 0:44–0:51 (7m) Deterministic checks -> response_checks.json. - 0:51–0:56 (5m) Finalize + run.py wiring -> final_responses.json. **MVP GREEN HERE.** - 0:56–1:00 (4m) README (required artifact) + quick swapped-fixture sanity run.
BUFFER RULE: validate.py and everything in are attempted ONLY if the MVP is green before 0:52. If behind at any checkpoint, cut the next stretch, never the MVP. HARD RULE: never let module ceremony (extra providers, config layers, reviewer) delay MVP-green.
plan.grading_surface:
NORTH STAR. Optimize the whole hour for these. (validate.py, if built, asserts them; otherwise verify manually before finishing.)
Execution model the evaluator uses: - Runs from a CLEAN CHECKOUT (no reliance on committed outputs). - May REPLACE tickets.json and policy_kb.json with equivalent fixtures (different IDs, wording, order, titles, counts) — NEVER hardcode any of these. - May DELETE generated artifacts first; pipeline must regenerate them. - Static/precomputed outputs are disqualified.
Checks that must hold after a run: 1. Required artifacts exist: triage.json, retrieval_results.json, draft_responses.json, response_checks.json, final_responses.json, llm_calls.jsonl. 2. Every artifact is valid JSON; llm_calls.jsonl is valid JSONL. 3. Inputs were read from disk: input_artifacts in llm_calls.jsonl point at real input paths; artifacts reflect current input contents (not embedded samples). 4. Exactly one triage record per ticket in triage.json AND in llm_calls.jsonl (stage=triage). 5. Exactly one draft record per ticket in draft_responses.json AND in llm_calls.jsonl (stage=drafting). 6. Retrieval preceded drafting: each draft's cited_policy_ids ⊆ that ticket's retrieved_policy_ids. 7. Every draft cites ≥1 retrieved policy; no out-of-set citations. 8. Failed deterministic checks -> final_status = needs_human_review. 9. Each llm_calls.jsonl record has ALL required fields (stage, ticket_id, timestamp, provider, model, prompt_hash, input_artifacts, output_artifact). 10. No emails/live actions; no secrets required to run. 11. Triage and drafting are NOT one call (distinct stage + prompt_hash per ticket-stage). 12. Determinism: two runs on identical inputs produce identical artifacts modulo timestamps.

requirements.functional: - Load + schema-validate tickets.json and policy_kb.json from configurable disk paths (default: repo root). - Enforce stages INIT -> INPUTS_LOADED -> TICKETS_PARSED -> KB_INDEXED -> TICKET_TRIAGED -> EVIDENCE_RETRIEVED -> RESPONSE_DRAFTED -> RESPONSE_CHECKED -> RESPONSE_FINALISED; illegal transitions raise; finals cannot be produced before RESPONSE_CHECKED. - Stage 1 Triage: one LLM call/ticket -> {ticket_id, category, priority, should_escalate, reason, missing_information[]}; category ∈ allowed set. Save triage.json. - Retrieval: deterministic code, top 2–3 policies/ticket, record score/ranking_explanation, include a tone/safety policy when relevant. Save retrieval_results.json. - Stage 2 Drafting: one LLM call/ticket; input = ticket + its triage + ONLY its retrieved snippets -> {ticket_id, subject, reply, cited_policy_ids, escalation_note}. No refund/approval/ expedite promises; if should_escalate, reply explains next step without claiming resolution; cite ≥1 retrieved policy. Save draft_responses.json. - Deterministic checks: missing citations; cited∉retrieved; banned phrases; empty/too-short reply; escalation mismatch -> {ticket_id, passed, issues[]}. Save response_checks.json. - Final pack: {ticket_id, category, priority, final_status(ready|needs_human_review), reply, supporting_policy_ids[], notes[]}. Fail -> needs_human_review; escalation may stay ready only if the draft clearly communicates escalation. Save final_responses.json. - Log every LLM call to llm_calls.jsonl with all required fields.
requirements.non_functional: - Clean checkout, ZERO secrets, ZERO pip installs: Python STDLIB ONLY. - Robust to swapped fixtures: never key logic to sample IDs, titles, wording, order, or counts. - Deterministic/reproducible: mock provider default; timestamps the only per-run variance. - Idempotent: `make clean && make run` rebuilds all artifacts identically (mod timestamps). - Logical stage separation into distinct modules and distinct artifact files. - Fail loud on malformed input with an actionable message.

plan.assumptions: PROCEED ON THESE if the open questions go unanswered in the first ~1 minute: - Time limit = 60 minutes. Python 3.10+ present in eval env; stdlib only. - A deterministic offline MockProvider is the acceptable DEFAULT "LLM"; a real provider is documented as swappable via config but NOT built in this box. - Inputs at repo root by default (./tickets.json, ./policy_kb.json), also CLI/config overridable. - Outputs written to ./out/ (configurable); input filenames stay at root. - Primary entrypoint is `python -m src.run`; Makefile is a convenience wrapper, not required. - "Equivalent fixtures" keep the same top-level schema (ticket_id, subject, message, ...; policy_id, title, content, tags). - English is the working language; non-`en` tickets are flagged in missing_information, not crashed.
plan.open_questions: ASK ALL AT ONCE, then PROCEED on if no quick reply (time-boxed to ~1 min): 1. Confirm 60-minute limit is correct for the plan (you stated 3h test but asked for a 60m plan — I built to 60m; say if you actually want 180m execution). 2. Is a deterministic mock-LLM default acceptable, or must a real LLM run (and if so, whose key, given "no secrets to run")? 3. Stdlib-only Python OK (no numpy/sklearn for retrieval)? 4. Where will swapped inputs be dropped — repo root (default) or a path arg?
plan.tech_stack: - Python 3.10+, STDLIB ONLY (json, hashlib, datetime, argparse, dataclasses, enum, re, pathlib). Justification: guarantees clean-checkout runs with no pip step; keyword/tag retrieval needs no ML libs. - Orchestration: hand-rolled Stage enum + StateMachine (NOT LangGraph) — zero deps, trivial to prove stage-gating, linear flow. Note LangGraph as the production alternative in README. - Retrieval: deterministic keyword + tag overlap scoring, stable tie-break by policy_id. - Config: single config/settings.json (no YAML dep) holding provider, model, paths, top_k, min_reply_len, banned_phrases, allowed_categories.
plan.architecture:
Flat, fast layout (fewer files than a 3h build, but stages stay separated): ./tickets.json # sample input (evaluator may replace) ./policy_kb.json # sample input (evaluator may replace) ./README.md ./Makefile # run / validate / clean (convenience) ./config/settings.json # provider, model, paths, top_k, min_reply_len, banned_phrases, categories ./src/ __init__.py run.py # entrypoint + orchestrator; drives the StateMachine end to end state.py # Stage(Enum) + StateMachine (assert legal transitions) io_utils.py # load_json, dump_json (ensure_ascii=false), append_jsonl, schema guards provider.py # Provider base + MockProvider; computes prompt_hash, writes llm_calls.jsonl triage.py # per-ticket Stage 1 loop retrieval.py # deterministic top-k + explanations drafting.py # per-ticket Stage 2 loop; injects ONLY retrieved snippets checks.py # deterministic guardrails finalize.py # status decision + final pack ./validate.py # STRETCH: mirrors grading surface; standalone `python validate.py` ./out/ # generated artifacts (gitignored)
Flow: run.py loads config -> loads+validates inputs (INPUTS_LOADED/TICKETS_PARSED) -> index KB (KB_INDEXED) -> triage loop (TICKET_TRIAGED) -> retrieval (EVIDENCE_RETRIEVED) -> drafting loop (RESPONSE_DRAFTED) -> checks (RESPONSE_CHECKED) -> finalize (RESPONSE_FINALISED). Provider injected; llm_calls.jsonl appended on every provider.complete(). Drafting receives ONLY that ticket's retrieved snippets — enforced by construction so citation-subset is guaranteed, not hoped for.
plan.deterministic_core: Computed in code, never delegated to the LLM: - StateMachine legality; refusal to finalize before RESPONSE_CHECKED. - Input loading, schema validation, KB tokenization/tag indexing. - Retrieval scoring (lowercased token overlap of ticket subject+message vs policy title+content+tags), stable ranking, top-k selection, tone/safety inclusion rule, ranking_explanation string. - Constraint that drafting sees ONLY retrieved snippets (by construction). - All checks: citations present; cited_policy_ids ⊆ retrieved_policy_ids; banned-phrase match (case-insensitive substring on config list); reply length ≥ min_reply_len; escalation mismatch (triage.should_escalate=true but reply lacks escalation language / escalation_note is null). - Final status: any check fail -> needs_human_review; escalation-as-ready only if escalation clearly stated. - prompt_hash = sha256 of the exact rendered prompt; llm_calls.jsonl assembly. The MockProvider is deterministic code standing in for the model; keep its stage logic generic (keyed to categories/keywords, NOT sample IDs) so swapped fixtures still yield valid structured output.
plan.task_breakdown: ORDERED. [tier] (est). First tasks immediately actionable. 1. [MVP] (4m) Repo skeleton, out/ + .gitignore, empty src modules, sample tickets.json/policy_kb.json at root, config/settings.json (provider="mock", model="mock-v1", paths, top_k=3, min_reply_len=40, banned_phrases list, allowed_categories list). 2. [MVP] (4m) state.py (Stage enum + StateMachine) + io_utils.py (load/dump json, append_jsonl, schema guards). 3. [MVP] (4m) provider.py: base + MockProvider (deterministic triage/drafting rules keyed to category keywords), prompt_hash, llm_calls.jsonl logging with all required fields. 4. [MVP] (10m) triage.py: per-ticket loop, one provider.complete() each -> triage.json. Verify one stage=triage record per ticket. 5. [MVP] (9m) retrieval.py: overlap scoring, top 2–3, ranking_explanation, safety-policy inclusion, fallback for zero-overlap tickets -> retrieval_results.json. MUST precede drafting. 6. [MVP] (10m) drafting.py: per-ticket loop, inject only retrieved snippets + triage -> draft_responses.json. cited_policy_ids ⊆ retrieved by construction; always ≥1 citation. 7. [MVP] (7m) checks.py: all five checks -> response_checks.json. 8. [MVP] (5m) finalize.py + run.py wiring: status logic -> final_responses.json. Full run green. 9. [MVP] (3m) README.md (required): run command, artifact-to-stage map, note mock-default + how to swap inputs. 10. [MVP] (2m) Swap sanity: replace inputs with a 2nd small fixture, rerun, confirm no crash + valid artifacts. -- MVP GREEN GATE (~0:56). Only proceed below if green and time remains. -- 11. [STRETCH] validate.py + Makefile (run/validate/clean) implementing the grading surface. 12. [STRETCH] Stage 3 reviewer -> review_results.json, folded into final status. 13. [STRETCH] Real provider (Anthropic temp=0 / Ollama) behind config — documented only if not built.
plan.acceptance_criteria: - `python -m src.run` (or `make run`) from a clean checkout with no env vars produces all six required artifacts in out/. - triage.json and draft_responses.json each hold exactly one record per input ticket. - llm_calls.jsonl holds one stage=triage and one stage=drafting record per ticket, each with all required fields and a nonempty prompt_hash. - Every draft's cited_policy_ids is a nonempty subset of that ticket's retrieved_policy_ids. - retrieval_results.json is produced before drafting reads any policy. - Any ticket failing a deterministic check has final_status = needs_human_review. - A should_escalate=true ticket is ready ONLY if its reply communicates the next step/escalation. - Running on a swapped fixture completes with no code changes and yields valid artifacts. - Two consecutive runs yield identical artifacts except timestamps.
plan.edge_cases: - Empty tickets or empty KB -> run completes, artifacts are valid empty structures. - Zero keyword/tag overlap -> retrieval returns top-k via fallback (include tone/safety policy) so drafting cites ≥1. - Fewer than 3 policies -> top_k clamps to available count. - Duplicate/missing ticket_id or missing required field -> fail loud during TICKETS_PARSED. - Malformed input JSON -> clear error, nonzero exit, no partial artifacts claimed valid. - Draft citing a non-retrieved policy -> caught by checks -> needs_human_review. - Banned phrase in reply -> check fail -> needs_human_review. - language != "en" -> note in missing_information, do not crash. - Non-ASCII names/content -> UTF-8 read/write, ensure_ascii=false on dump.
plan.test_plan: Time-boxed to manual + light checks at 60 min (no formal unit suite unless MVP is green early): - Full run on sample fixtures -> all six artifacts present, valid JSON, per-ticket separation confirmed by eye/grep. - Grep llm_calls.jsonl: one triage + one drafting record per ticket_id. - Swap-fixture run (renamed IDs, reordered, different titles, different count) -> completes, artifacts valid. - Reproducibility: run twice, diff out/ excluding timestamp fields -> identical. - Negative: inject a banned phrase into a mock reply -> that ticket becomes needs_human_review. - If validate.py built: `python validate.py` exits 0 on sample and swapped fixture.
plan.required_artifacts: Inputs (read from disk, evaluator-replaceable): tickets.json, policy_kb.json Generated (in out/): triage.json, retrieval_results.json, draft_responses.json, response_checks.json, final_responses.json, llm_calls.jsonl Docs/config: README.md, config/settings.json Conditional (stretch only): validate.py, Makefile, review_results.json
plan.definition_of_done: - MUST items 1–5 implemented; all six core artifacts regenerate from disk on a clean checkout with no secrets. - Manual verification confirms per-ticket separation, citation-subset constraint, and needs_human_review flips. - Runs cleanly on both the sample fixtures and one swapped fixture with no code changes. - Determinism verified (double-run diff clean modulo timestamps). - README documents the run command, artifact-to-stage map, mock default, and how to swap inputs/provider. - STRETCH items attempted ONLY after the above is green.
plan.stretch_goals: Attempt only if MVP is green before 0:52, in this order (highest ROI first): - validate.py + `make validate` mirroring the full grading surface. - Stage 3 reviewer folded into final status. - Real provider (Anthropic temp=0 or Ollama) behind config with response caching for reproducibility.
plan.brainstorming: STEP 0 — Before writing ANY code, invoke the brainstorming skill (superpowers toolkit), FAST: 1. Ask the four as ONE batch and wait ~1 minute for a reply. 2. If no reply, PROCEED immediately on the defaults — do not stall a 60-minute box. 3. State the locked scope: MUST items 1–5 + required artifacts + minimal README this hour; SHOULD/STRETCH only after MVP-green (~0:56). 4. Restate the checklist we are optimizing for, then work in order. Keep the MVP-green gate sacred: if behind at any checkpoint, cut the next stretch, never the MVP.
